signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.4; 160.00 is the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — 157.00 is the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 vs retail 89% short — institutional vs retail divergence at extreme. HV30 at 6.2%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 7.4% — options pricing a 1.2 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.21: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. Late-trend caution signal. A LF delta turning positive or retail reducing to shift the read.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp GBP carry neutral; GBP LF net long +28,426. GBP/USD at 1.3502; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation. ATM IV 9.5% near HV, 25d RR flat — options neutral.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT + Technical
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −17,090, long%=21.7%. NZD/USD at 0.5908; 0.6000 is the next resistance on a continuation. NZD/USD vs NZX 50: corr=+0.648 (z=+2.16σ) — NZD/USD decoupling from equities.
